settings.title: An Open Evening for Autumn Couples
settings.excerpt: One relaxed evening with a few of our favourite vendors, no hard sell and good cake.
settings.body:
Planning a wedding can feel like meeting forty strangers in forty separate meetings. We wanted to make at least part of it feel like a nice night out.

This June we are hosting an open evening for couples marrying in autumn. A florist, a baker, and a celebrant we love will be there to chat, with samples to taste and zero pressure to commit.

What the evening is like

It is informal by design. Rather than a row of sales tables, it is more like a relaxed gathering where you can wander, taste, and ask the questions you have been saving up. Nobody will be chasing you for a deposit.

  • A florist to talk seasonal autumn flowers with.
  • A baker, with cake to actually taste.
  • A celebrant who can answer the ceremony questions.

Come with your partner, come with your questions, and leave with a clearer picture and a slice of cake. Spaces are limited, so let us know if you would like to come along.

Why we do it this way

Meeting three vendors we already trust in one easy evening saves you weeks of separate appointments, and it lets you see how people get on before anyone signs anything. The right fit matters as much as the price.

If autumn is your season, this is a gentle way to start. Send us a note to hold a space, bring whatever questions are keeping you up at night, and we will make the rest of the evening pleasant.
